| COR-2397 | 2026-04-18 | Identify the CPC technology areas with the highest exponential moving average of patent filings each year (smoothing factor 0.2), and return only the CPC group codes at level 5 whose best year is 2022. | wrong_table | — | MCP RPC error: {'code': -32000, 'message': 'relation "publication_database.publicationinfo" does not exist\nLINE 7:         publication_database.publicationinfo\n                ^\n'} | WITH YearlyFilings AS (     SELECT         CAST(EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CAST(filing_date AS DATE)) AS INTEGER) AS filing_year,         cpc AS cpc_code,         COUNT(*) AS filing_count     FROM         publicationinfo     WHERE filing_date IS NOT NULL     GROUP BY         1, 2 ), RankedFilings AS (     SELECT         filing_year,         cpc_code,         filing_count,         RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY cpc_code ORDER BY filing_year) as rank_year     FROM         YearlyFilings ), LaggedFilings AS (     SELECT         filing_year,         cpc_code,         filing_count,         LAG(filing_count, 1, 0) OVER (PARTITION BY cpc_code ORDER BY filing_year) AS previous_filing_count     FROM         RankedFilings ), EMAFilings AS (     SELECT         filing_year,         cpc_code,         filing_count,         (0.2 * filing_count + (1 - 0.2) * previous_filing_count) AS ema     FROM         LaggedFilings ), MostRecentEMA AS (     SELECT         cpc_code,         MAX(filing_year) AS max_filing_year     FROM EMAFilings     GROUP BY cpc_code ) SELECT     ema.cpc_code FROM     EMAFilings ema INNER JOIN     MostRecentEMA mre ON ema.cpc_code = mre.cpc_code AND ema.filing_year = mre.max_filing_year WHERE ema.filing_year = 2022 AND LENGTH(ema.cpc_code) = 5 ORDER BY     ema.ema DESC; | pending |

| COR-2399 | 2026-04-18 | Identify the CPC technology areas with the highest exponential moving average of patent filings each year (smoothing factor 0.2), and return only the CPC group codes at level 5 whose best year is 2022. | syntax_error | — | MCP RPC error: {'code': -32000, 'message': 'syntax error at end of input\nLINE 1: WITH YearlyFilings AS\n                             ^\n'} | WITH YearlyFilings AS (     SELECT         EXTRACT(YEAR FROM publication_date) AS filing_year,         cpc,         COUNT(*) AS filing_count     FROM         publicationinfo     WHERE publication_date IS NOT NULL     GROUP BY         1, 2 ), RankedFilings AS (     SELECT         filing_year,         cpc,         filing_count,         ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY cpc ORDER BY filing_year) AS rn     FROM         YearlyFilings ), EMAFilings AS (     SELECT         filing_year,         cpc,         filing_count,         CASE             WHEN rn = 1 THEN filing_count             ELSE LAG(ema, 1, filing_count) OVER (PARTITION BY cpc ORDER BY filing_year) * 0.8 + filing_count * 0.2         END AS ema     FROM         RankedFilings ), BestYear AS (     SELECT         cpc,         filing_year,         ema,         ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY cpc ORDER BY ema DESC) AS rn     FROM         EMAFilings ) SELECT     LEFT(cd.symbol, 5) FROM     BestYear byy JOIN     cpc_definition cd ON byy.cpc = cd.symbol WHERE     byy.rn = 1     AND byy.filing_year = 2022 GROUP BY LEFT(cd.symbol, 5) ORDER BY SUM(byy.ema) DESC LIMIT 10; | pending |

| COR-2400 | 2026-04-18 | Find the CPC technology areas in Germany with the highest exponential moving average of patent filings each year (smoothing factor 0.1) for patents granted in the second half of 2019. Include the full title, CPC group code, and the best year for each CPC group at level 4. | wrong_table | — | MCP RPC error: {'code': -32000, 'message': 'no such table: cpc_definition'} | WITH YearlyFilings AS (   SELECT     STRFTIME('%Y', T1.filing_date) AS filing_year,     T1.cpc AS cpc_code,     COUNT(T1.cpc) AS filing_count   FROM publicationinfo AS T1   WHERE     T1.country_code = 'DE' AND T1.grant_date LIKE '%2019%' AND CAST(SUBSTR(T1.grant_date, 6, 2) AS INTEGER) BETWEEN 7 AND 12   GROUP BY     filing_year,     cpc_code ), EMA AS (   SELECT     filing_year,     cpc_code,     filing_count,     (       SELECT         SUM(filing_count * POWER(0.1, (           CAST(T2.filing_year AS INTEGER) - CAST(T1.filing_year AS INTEGER)         )))       FROM YearlyFilings AS T2       WHERE         T2.cpc_code = T1.cpc_code AND CAST(T2.filing_year AS INTEGER) <= CAST(T1.filing_year AS INTEGER)     ) AS exponential_moving_average   FROM YearlyFilings AS T1 ), RankedEMA AS (   SELECT     filing_year,     cpc_code,     exponential_moving_average,     RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY cpc_code ORDER BY exponential_moving_average DESC) AS rank_num   FROM EMA ) SELECT   REA.filing_year AS best_year,   REA.exponential_moving_average,   REA.cpc_code FROM RankedEMA AS REA WHERE   REA.rank_num = 1 ORDER BY   REA.exponential_moving_average DESC; | pending |

| COR-2502 | 2026-04-18 | In the past four months, which agent had the lowest average handle time for those processing more than one case? Return only the Id of the agent.  ## Handle Time Policy - Definition: The duration taken to close a case. Specifically, it is the time from when a case is opened to when it is closed. - In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to both the first agent that the case was first assigned to and the agent that the case was transferred to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would not filter this agent out. - When computing handle time, we do not compute handle time for cases that have been transferred to other agents. - For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.  ## Today's date: 2023-09-02 | syntax_error | — | MCP RPC error: {'code': -32000, 'message': 'syntax error at or near "case"\nLINE 7:     FROM case\n                 ^\n'} | SELECT ownerid FROM (     SELECT          TRIM(LEADING '#' FROM ownerid) AS ownerid,         AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (CAST(closeddate AS TIMESTAMP) - CAST(createddate AS TIMESTAMP)))) AS avg_handle_time,         COUNT(id) AS case_count     FROM "case"     WHERE          TRIM(status) = 'Closed'         AND CAST(closeddate AS TIMESTAMP) >= (CAST('2023-09-02' AS TIMESTAMP) - INTERVAL '4 months')         AND id NOT IN (             SELECT caseid              FROM casehistory              WHERE field = 'Owner'              GROUP BY caseid              HAVING COUNT(*) > 1         )     GROUP BY TRIM(LEADING '#' FROM ownerid)     HAVING COUNT(id) > 1 ) AS agent_stats ORDER BY avg_handle_time ASC LIMIT 1; | pending |

| COR-2503 | 2026-04-18 | In the past four months, which agent had the lowest average handle time for those processing more than one case? Return only the Id of the agent.  ## Handle Time Policy - Definition: The duration taken to close a case. Specifically, it is the time from when a case is opened to when it is closed. - In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to both the first agent that the case was first assigned to and the agent that the case was transferred to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would not filter this agent out. - When computing handle time, we do not compute handle time for cases that have been transferred to other agents. - For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.  ## Today's date: 2023-09-02 | wrong_table | — | MCP RPC error: {'code': -32000, 'message': 'relation "case" does not exist\nLINE 7:     FROM "case"\n                 ^\n'} | SELECT ownerid FROM (     SELECT          TRIM(LEADING '#' FROM ownerid) AS ownerid,         AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (CAST(closeddate AS TIMESTAMP) - CAST(createddate AS TIMESTAMP)))) AS avg_handle_time,         COUNT(id) AS case_count     FROM "Case"     WHERE          TRIM(status) = 'Closed'         AND CAST(closeddate AS TIMESTAMP) >= (CAST('2023-09-02' AS TIMESTAMP) - INTERVAL '4 months')         AND id NOT IN (             SELECT caseid              FROM casehistory              WHERE field = 'Owner'              GROUP BY caseid              HAVING COUNT(*) > 0         )     GROUP BY TRIM(LEADING '#' FROM ownerid)     HAVING COUNT(id) > 1 ) AS agent_stats ORDER BY avg_handle_time ASC LIMIT 1; | pending |

| COR-2504 | 2026-04-18 | In the past four months, which agent had the lowest average handle time for those processing more than one case? Return only the Id of the agent.  ## Handle Time Policy - Definition: The duration taken to close a case. Specifically, it is the time from when a case is opened to when it is closed. - In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to both the first agent that the case was first assigned to and the agent that the case was transferred to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would not filter this agent out. - When computing handle time, we do not compute handle time for cases that have been transferred to other agents. - For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.  ## Today's date: 2023-09-02 | wrong_table | — | MCP RPC error: {'code': -32000, 'message': 'relation "casehistory" does not exist\nLINE 13:             FROM casehistory \n                          ^\n'} | SELECT ownerid FROM (     SELECT          TRIM(LEADING '#' FROM ownerid) AS ownerid,         AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (CAST(closeddate AS TIMESTAMP) - CAST(createddate AS TIMESTAMP)))) AS avg_handle_time,         COUNT(id) AS case_count     FROM "Case"     WHERE          TRIM(status) = 'Closed'         AND CAST(closeddate AS TIMESTAMP) >= (CAST('2023-09-02' AS TIMESTAMP) - INTERVAL '4 months')         AND TRIM(LEADING '#' FROM id) NOT IN (             SELECT TRIM(LEADING '#' FROM caseid)             FROM case_history              WHERE TRIM(field) = 'ownerid' OR TRIM(field) = 'Owner'             GROUP BY TRIM(LEADING '#' FROM caseid)         )     GROUP BY TRIM(LEADING '#' FROM ownerid)     HAVING COUNT(id) > 1 ) AS agent_stats ORDER BY avg_handle_time ASC LIMIT 1; | pending |

| COR-2508 | 2026-04-18 | Who had the quickest average turnaround from opening to closing opportunities among agents in April 2023? Return only the Id of the agent.  ## Sales Cycle Policy - Definition: The sales cycle is measured as the number of days between an opportunity's creation date and the company signed date on the corresponding contract.  ## Today's date: 2024-09-12 | wrong_table | — | MCP RPC error: {'code': -32000, 'message': 'Catalog Error: Table with name sales_pipeline does not exist!\nDid you mean "pg_depend"?\n\nLINE 3: FROM sales_pipeline\n             ^'} | SELECT     OwnerId FROM crmarenapro.SALES_PIPELINE WHERE     COALESCE(         TRY_STRPTIME(CreatedDate, '%B %d, %Y at %I:%M %p'),         TRY_STRPTIME(CreatedDate, '%d %b %Y, %H:%M')     ) >= '2023-04-01'     AND COALESCE(         TRY_STRPTIME(CreatedDate, '%B %d, %Y at %I:%M %p'),         TRY_STRPTIME(CreatedDate, '%d %b %Y, %H:%M')     ) < '2023-05-01'     AND CompanySignedDate IS NOT NULL GROUP BY OwnerId ORDER BY     AVG(         DATEDIFF(             'day',             COALESCE(TRY_STRPTIME(CreatedDate, '%B %d, %Y at %I:%M %p'), TRY_STRPTIME(CreatedDate, '%d %b %Y, %H:%M')),             COALESCE(TRY_STRPTIME(CompanySignedDate, '%B %d, %Y at %I:%M %p'), TRY_STRPTIME(CompanySignedDate, '%d %b %Y, %H:%M'))         )     ) ASC LIMIT 1; | pending |

| COR-2535 | 2026-04-18 | Identify the agent with the fewest transfer counts in the last 4 quarters among those who handled more than 0 cases. Return only the Id of the agent.  ## Transfer Count Policy - Definition: The number of instances a case was reassigned or transferred from one agent to another. Each transfer from agent A to agent B adds to the transfer count for agent A. - In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to both the first agent that the case was first assigned to and the agent that the case was transferred to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would not filter this agent out. - For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.  ## Today's date: 2023-04-10 | wrong_table | — | MCP RPC error: {'code': -32000, 'message': 'relation "CaseHistory" does not exist\nLINE 6:     FROM "CaseHistory"\n                 ^\n'} | SELECT TRIM(LEADING '#' FROM "OwnerId") FROM "Case" WHERE "CreatedDate" >= '2022-04-01' AND "CreatedDate" < '2023-04-10' GROUP BY TRIM(LEADING '#' FROM "OwnerId") HAVING COUNT(*) > 0 ORDER BY SUM(CASE WHEN "IsTransferred" = 'true' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) ASC LIMIT 1; | pending |

| COR-2550 | 2026-04-18 | Considering only the latest release versions for each distinct NPM package, which packages are the top 5 most popular based on the Github star number, as well as their versions? | unknown | — | No MCP tool mapped for db_type 'package_database' | WITH LatestNPMReleases AS (   SELECT Name, Version   FROM packageinfo   WHERE System = 'NPM'      AND JSON_EXTRACT_SCALAR(VersionInfo, '$.IsRelease') = 'true' ), ProjectStars AS (   SELECT      ProjectName,     CAST(REPLACE(REGEXP_EXTRACT(Project_Information, 'stars count of ([\d,]+)', 1), ',', '') AS INTEGER) as stars   FROM project_info   WHERE Project_Information LIKE '%stars count of%' ) SELECT    ppv.Name,    ppv.Version,    ps.stars FROM project_packageversion ppv JOIN LatestNPMReleases lnr ON ppv.Name = lnr.Name AND ppv.Version = lnr.Version JOIN ProjectStars ps ON ppv.ProjectName = ps.ProjectName WHERE ppv.System = 'NPM' ORDER BY ps.stars DESC LIMIT 5; | pending |

| COR-2551 | 2026-04-18 | Considering only the latest release versions for each distinct NPM package, which packages are the top 5 most popular based on the Github star number, as well as their versions? | unknown | — | No MCP tool mapped for db_type 'package_database' | WITH LatestNPMReleases AS (   SELECT Name, Version   FROM packageinfo   WHERE System = 'NPM'      AND IsLatest = TRUE ), ProjectStars AS (   SELECT      ProjectName,     CAST(StarsCount AS INTEGER) as stars   FROM project_info   WHERE StarsCount IS NOT NULL ) SELECT    lnr.Name,    lnr.Version,    ps.stars FROM LatestNPMReleases lnr JOIN project_packageversion ppv ON lnr.Name = ppv.Name AND lnr.Version = ppv.Version AND ppv.System = 'NPM' JOIN ProjectStars ps ON ppv.ProjectName = ps.ProjectName ORDER BY ps.stars DESC LIMIT 5; | pending |

| COR-2552 | 2026-04-18 | Considering only the latest release versions for each distinct NPM package, which packages are the top 5 most popular based on the Github star number, as well as their versions? | unknown | — | No MCP tool mapped for db_type 'package_database' | WITH LatestNPMReleases AS (     SELECT Name, Version     FROM packageinfo     WHERE System = 'NPM'      AND IsLatest = TRUE ), ProjectStars AS (     SELECT          ProjectName,         CAST(StarsCount AS INTEGER) as stars     FROM project_info ) SELECT      lnr.Name,      lnr.Version,      ps.stars FROM LatestNPMReleases lnr JOIN project_packageversion ppv ON lnr.Name = ppv.Name AND lnr.Version = ppv.Version AND ppv.System = 'NPM' JOIN ProjectStars ps ON ppv.ProjectName = ps.ProjectName ORDER BY ps.stars DESC LIMIT 5; | pending |

| COR-2553 | 2026-04-18 | Considering only the latest release versions for each distinct NPM package, which packages are the top 5 most popular based on the Github star number, as well as their versions? | unknown | — | No MCP tool mapped for db_type 'project_database' | WITH latest_versions AS (   SELECT      System,      Name,      MAX(Version) as LatestVersion   FROM project_packageversion   WHERE System = 'NPM'   GROUP BY System, Name ), project_stats AS (   SELECT     REGEXP_EXTRACT(Project_Information, 'project ([a-zA-Z0-9._-]+/[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+)', 1) AS ProjectName,     CAST(REPLACE(COALESCE(       NULLIF(REGEXP_EXTRACT(Project_Information, '([\d,]+) stars', 1), ''),       NULLIF(REGEXP_EXTRACT(Project_Information, 'stars count of ([\d,]+)', 1), '')     ), ',', '') AS BIGINT) AS stars   FROM project_info ) SELECT    ppv.Name,    ppv.Version,    pn.stars FROM project_packageversion ppv JOIN latest_versions lv ON ppv.System = lv.System AND ppv.Name = lv.Name AND ppv.Version = lv.LatestVersion JOIN project_stats pn ON ppv.ProjectName = pn.ProjectName WHERE pn.stars IS NOT NULL ORDER BY pn.stars DESC LIMIT 5; | pending |

| COR-2554 | 2026-04-18 | Considering only the latest release versions for each distinct NPM package, which packages are the top 5 most popular based on the Github star number, as well as their versions? | unknown | — | No MCP tool mapped for db_type 'project_database' | WITH latest_versions AS (   SELECT      System,      Name,      MAX(Version) as LatestVersion   FROM project_packageversion   WHERE System = 'NPM'   GROUP BY System, Name ), project_stars AS (   SELECT     ProjectName,     CAST(REPLACE(REGEXP_EXTRACT(Project_Information, 'stars count of ([\d,]+)', 1), ',', '') AS BIGINT) AS stars   FROM project_info ) SELECT    ppv.Name,    ppv.Version,    ps.stars FROM project_packageversion ppv JOIN latest_versions lv    ON ppv.System = lv.System    AND ppv.Name = lv.Name    AND ppv.Version = lv.LatestVersion JOIN project_stars ps    ON ppv.ProjectName = ps.ProjectName WHERE ppv.System = 'NPM' ORDER BY ps.stars DESC LIMIT 5; | pending |

| COR-2555 | 2026-04-18 | Considering only the latest release versions for each distinct NPM package, which packages are the top 5 most popular based on the Github star number, as well as their versions? | unknown | — | No MCP tool mapped for db_type 'project_database' | WITH LatestNPMVersions AS (   SELECT      System,      Name,      MAX(Version) as LatestVersion   FROM project_packageversion   WHERE System = 'NPM'   GROUP BY System, Name ), StarParsing AS (   SELECT     ProjectName,     CAST(REPLACE(REGEXP_EXTRACT(Project_Information, 'stars count of ([\d,]+)', 1), ',', '') AS BIGINT) AS GitHubStars   FROM project_info ) SELECT    ppv.Name,    ppv.Version,    sp.GitHubStars FROM project_packageversion ppv JOIN LatestNPMVersions lnv    ON ppv.System = lnv.System    AND ppv.Name = lnv.Name    AND ppv.Version = lnv.LatestVersion JOIN StarParsing sp    ON ppv.ProjectName = sp.ProjectName ORDER BY sp.GitHubStars DESC LIMIT 5; | pending |

| COR-2559 | 2026-04-18 | Considering only the latest release versions for each distinct NPM package, which packages are the top 5 most popular based on the Github star number, as well as their versions? | unknown | — | No MCP tool mapped for db_type 'package_database' | WITH LatestVersions AS (     SELECT System, Name, MAX(Version) as MaxVersion     FROM packageinfo     WHERE System = 'NPM'     GROUP BY System, Name ), LatestPackageDetails AS (     SELECT p.System, p.Name, p.Version     FROM packageinfo p     JOIN LatestVersions lv ON p.System = lv.System AND p.Name = lv.Name AND p.Version = lv.MaxVersion     WHERE p.System = 'NPM' ), ProjectStars AS (     SELECT          ProjectName,         CAST(REPLACE(REGEXP_EXTRACT(Project_Information, 'stars count of ([\d,]+)', 1), ',', '') AS INTEGER) as star_count     FROM project_info ) SELECT      lpd.Name,      lpd.Version,      ps.star_count FROM LatestPackageDetails lpd JOIN project_packageversion ppv ON lpd.System = ppv.System AND lpd.Name = ppv.Name AND lpd.Version = ppv.Version JOIN ProjectStars ps ON ppv.ProjectName = ps.ProjectName WHERE ps.star_count IS NOT NULL ORDER BY ps.star_count DESC LIMIT 5; | pending |

| COR-2560 | 2026-04-18 | Considering only the latest release versions for each distinct NPM package, which packages are the top 5 most popular based on the Github star number, as well as their versions? | unknown | — | No MCP tool mapped for db_type 'package_database' | WITH LatestVersions AS (     SELECT System, Name, MAX(Version) as MaxVersion     FROM packageinfo     WHERE System = 'NPM'     GROUP BY Name ), LatestPackageDetails AS (     SELECT p.System, p.Name, p.Version     FROM packageinfo p     INNER JOIN LatestVersions lv ON p.Name = lv.Name AND p.Version = lv.MaxVersion     WHERE p.System = 'NPM' ), ProjectStars AS (     SELECT          ProjectName,         CAST(REPLACE(REGEXP_EXTRACT(Project_Information, 'stars count of ([\d,]+)', 1), ',', '') AS INTEGER) as star_count     FROM project_info ) SELECT      lpd.Name,      lpd.Version,      ps.star_count FROM LatestPackageDetails lpd JOIN project_packageversion ppv ON lpd.System = ppv.System AND lpd.Name = ppv.Name AND lpd.Version = ppv.Version JOIN ProjectStars ps ON ppv.ProjectName = ps.ProjectName WHERE ps.star_count IS NOT NULL ORDER BY ps.star_count DESC LIMIT 5; | pending |

| COR-2561 | 2026-04-18 | Considering only the latest release versions for each distinct NPM package, which packages are the top 5 most popular based on the Github star number, as well as their versions? | unknown | — | No MCP tool mapped for db_type 'package_database' | WITH LatestVersions AS (     SELECT System, Name, MAX(Version) as LatestVersion     FROM packageinfo     WHERE System = 'NPM'     GROUP BY Name ), ProjectStars AS (     SELECT          ProjectName,         CAST(REPLACE(REGEXP_EXTRACT(Project_Information, 'stars count of ([\d,]+)', 1), ',', '') AS INTEGER) as star_count     FROM project_info ) SELECT      lv.Name,      lv.LatestVersion as Version,      ps.star_count FROM LatestVersions lv JOIN project_packageversion ppv ON lv.System = ppv.System AND lv.Name = ppv.Name AND lv.LatestVersion = ppv.Version JOIN ProjectStars ps ON ppv.ProjectName = ps.ProjectName WHERE ps.star_count IS NOT NULL ORDER BY ps.star_count DESC LIMIT 5; | pending |

| COR-2562 | 2026-04-18 | Considering only the latest release versions for each distinct NPM package, which packages are the top 5 most popular based on the Github star number, as well as their versions? | unknown | — | No MCP tool mapped for db_type 'project_database' | WITH RankedPackages AS (     SELECT          ppv.Name,          ppv.Version,          ps.ProjectName,         CAST(REPLACE(REGEXP_EXTRACT(pi.Project_Information, '(?i)(\d+)\s+stars', 1), ',', '') AS BIGINT) as Stars,         ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY ppv.Name ORDER BY ppv.Version DESC) as rank     FROM project_packageversion ppv     JOIN packageinfo p ON ppv.System = p.System AND ppv.Name = p.Name AND ppv.Version = p.Version     JOIN project_info pi ON ppv.ProjectName = REGEXP_EXTRACT(pi.Project_Information, '(?i)project\s+([^, ]+)', 1)     WHERE ppv.System = 'NPM'        AND p.VersionInfo LIKE '%"IsRelease": true%' ) SELECT      Name,      Version,      Stars FROM RankedPackages WHERE rank = 1 ORDER BY Stars DESC LIMIT 5; | pending |

| COR-2600 | 2026-04-18 | In the past four months, which agent had the lowest average handle time for those processing more than one case? Return only the Id of the agent.  ## Handle Time Policy - Definition: The duration taken to close a case. Specifically, it is the time from when a case is opened to when it is closed. - In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to both the first agent that the case was first assigned to and the agent that the case was transferred to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would not filter this agent out. - When computing handle time, we do not compute handle time for cases that have been transferred to other agents. - For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.  ## Today's date: 2023-09-02 | wrong_table | — | MCP RPC error: {'code': -32000, 'message': 'column "OwnerId" does not exist\nLINE 1: SELECT LTRIM("OwnerId", \'#\')\n                     ^\nHINT:  Perhaps you meant to reference the column "Case.ownerid".\n'} | SELECT LTRIM(ownerid, '#') FROM "Case" WHERE createddate >= '2023-09-02'::date - INTERVAL '4 months'   AND status = 'Closed'   AND LTRIM(id, '#') NOT IN (       SELECT LTRIM(caseid, '#')       FROM "CaseHistory"       WHERE field = 'Owner'       GROUP BY LTRIM(caseid, '#')       HAVING COUNT(*) > 1   ) GROUP BY LTRIM(ownerid, '#') HAVING COUNT(*) > 1 ORDER BY AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (closeddate - createddate))) ASC LIMIT 1; | pending |

| COR-2642 | 2026-04-18 | Identify the agent with the fewest transfer counts in the last 4 quarters among those who handled more than 0 cases. Return only the Id of the agent.  ## Transfer Count Policy - Definition: The number of instances a case was reassigned or transferred from one agent to another. Each transfer from agent A to agent B adds to the transfer count for agent A. - In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to both the first agent that the case was first assigned to and the agent that the case was transferred to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would not filter this agent out. - For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.  ## Today's date: 2023-04-10 | wrong_table | — | MCP RPC error: {'code': -32000, 'message': 'relation "casehistory" does not exist\nLINE 6:     FROM CaseHistory\n                 ^\n'} | SELECT      TRIM(LEADING '#' FROM "Case".OwnerId) AS CleanOwnerId FROM "Case" LEFT JOIN (     SELECT          TRIM(LEADING '#' FROM "CaseHistory".OwnerId) AS CleanOwnerId,         COUNT(*) AS TransferCount     FROM "CaseHistory"     WHERE "CaseHistory".Field = 'Owner'       AND "CaseHistory".CreatedDate >= (DATE '2023-04-10' - INTERVAL '1 year')     GROUP BY CleanOwnerId ) AS Transfers ON TRIM(LEADING '#' FROM "Case".OwnerId) = Transfers.CleanOwnerId WHERE "Case".CreatedDate >= (DATE '2023-04-10' - INTERVAL '1 year') GROUP BY TRIM(LEADING '#' FROM "Case".OwnerId), Transfers.TransferCount HAVING COUNT(*) > 0 ORDER BY COALESCE(Transfers.TransferCount, 0) ASC LIMIT 1; | pending |

| COR-2643 | 2026-04-18 | Identify the agent with the fewest transfer counts in the last 4 quarters among those who handled more than 0 cases. Return only the Id of the agent.  ## Transfer Count Policy - Definition: The number of instances a case was reassigned or transferred from one agent to another. Each transfer from agent A to agent B adds to the transfer count for agent A. - In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to both the first agent that the case was first assigned to and the agent that the case was transferred to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would not filter this agent out. - For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.  ## Today's date: 2023-04-10 | wrong_table | — | MCP RPC error: {'code': -32000, 'message': 'relation "CaseHistory" does not exist\nLINE 8:     FROM "CaseHistory"\n                 ^\n'} | SELECT      TRIM(LEADING '#' FROM "Case"."OwnerId") AS CleanOwnerId FROM "Case" LEFT JOIN (     SELECT          TRIM(LEADING '#' FROM "CaseHistory"."CaseId") AS HistoryCaseId,         TRIM(LEADING '#' FROM "CaseHistory"."OldValue") AS TransferringAgentId     FROM "CaseHistory"     WHERE "CaseHistory"."Field" = 'Owner'       AND "CaseHistory"."CreatedDate" >= (DATE '2023-04-10' - INTERVAL '1 year') ) AS Transfers ON TRIM(LEADING '#' FROM "Case"."Id") = Transfers.HistoryCaseId      AND TRIM(LEADING '#' FROM "Case"."OwnerId") = Transfers.TransferringAgentId WHERE "Case"."CreatedDate" >= (DATE '2023-04-10' - INTERVAL '1 year') GROUP BY TRIM(LEADING '#' FROM "Case"."OwnerId") HAVING COUNT("Case"."Id") > 0 ORDER BY COUNT(Transfers.TransferringAgentId) ASC LIMIT 1; | pending |

| COR-2644 | 2026-04-18 | Identify the agent with the fewest transfer counts in the last 4 quarters among those who handled more than 0 cases. Return only the Id of the agent.  ## Transfer Count Policy - Definition: The number of instances a case was reassigned or transferred from one agent to another. Each transfer from agent A to agent B adds to the transfer count for agent A. - In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to both the first agent that the case was first assigned to and the agent that the case was transferred to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would not filter this agent out. - For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.  ## Today's date: 2023-04-10 | wrong_table | — | MCP RPC error: {'code': -32000, 'message': 'relation "CaseHistory" does not exist\nLINE 8:     FROM "CaseHistory"\n                 ^\n'} | WITH CaseHandling AS (     SELECT TRIM(LEADING '#' FROM "OwnerId") AS AgentId, COUNT("Id") as handled_count     FROM "Case"     GROUP BY TRIM(LEADING '#' FROM "OwnerId") ), Transfers AS (     SELECT          TRIM(LEADING '#' FROM "CreatedById") AS TransferringAgentId,         COUNT(*) AS transfer_count     FROM "Case"     WHERE "IsTransferred" = 'true'       AND "CreatedDate" >= (DATE '2023-04-10' - INTERVAL '1 year')     GROUP BY TRIM(LEADING '#' FROM "CreatedById") ) SELECT      h.AgentId FROM CaseHandling h LEFT JOIN Transfers t ON h.AgentId = t.TransferringAgentId WHERE h.handled_count > 0 ORDER BY COALESCE(t.transfer_count, 0) ASC LIMIT 1; | pending |
